extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT won Stage 5 with superior positioning through late technical corners, outsprinting BENNETT after BOASSON HAGEN and GILBERT's late attack nearly succeeded.
tactical_patterns: BOASSON HAGEN and GILBERT attacked inside 1km on technical corners, forcing sprinter teams to chase through dangerous turns.
Bora-Hansgrohe hit the front at 4km despite BENNETT requesting 2km positioning, compromising his sprint train execution.
Breakaway riders DE MARCHI and ROSSETTO counter-attacked in final 2km when peloton chased, exploiting sprinter team disorganization.
VAN AERT positioned behind BENNETT's leadout through technical corners rather than relying on own team, revealing tactical flexibility.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Won second consecutive stage beating pure sprinters, demonstrating roadman sprint capability beyond cyclocross reputation
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: Second place despite team hitting front 4km out instead of requested 2km, suggesting sprint train timing issues
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: Third after opening sprint early, positioning strong but lacked final acceleration against crossover specialists
rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: positive: Nearly stole stage with sub-1km technical corner attack alongside GILBERT, showing selective sprint race reading
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: Launched late counter with BOASSON HAGEN through corners, demonstrating classics rider opportunism in bunch sprint scenario
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Yellow jersey defended comfortably on sprinter terrain, team conserved energy by letting sprinter squads control pace
